title: Achievements
text:
Awards
Below are some of my academic awards, beginning with the recognition I received in 2017 for achieving the highest entrance examination score.





I went on to achieve the highest GPA in the program, a reflection of consistent performance across all subjects.





This excellence was honored with the university’s Top Graduate Award, often referred to as the Valedictorian prize.





Finally, I also received several other recognitions for my academic contributions and professional work, which further shaped my growth as both a researcher and an educator.

title: Research
text:
RStudio
R is one of the most frequently used tools. I use it to analyse experimental data, estimate Bayesian networks, build predictive models, and apply techniques such as Elastic Net and Random Forest. Below are the methods I am proficient in.

title: Todd Cao - Lecturer in Marketing and Management
text:
Welcome to my little corner of the world
Hi, I’m Todd Cao.
This website is where I collect the things that keep me curious: research, teaching, marketing, business, and the guitar riff.
By day, I teach and conduct research. My work explores how people think, choose, and interact with artificial intelligence. Along the way, I have been fortunate to earn a PhD in Management from the University of Southern Queensland and am completing a second PhD in Business Administration at Open University, Vietnam.
Over the years, I have taught courses ranging from Business and Management Fundamentals to Marketing, Public Relations, Crisis Management, and Media Audience Research. I enjoy helping students connect theory with the messy realities of the world outside the classroom.
Feel free to wander around. You will find my research, teaching materials, projects, and a few stories from the journey.


Please explore my little world

title: Publications
text:
Welcome to my bookstore
Publishing papers is one of the things that occupies a large part of my world. I am fascinated by how people make decisions when technology begins to blur the line between human and machine. Much of my work explores how consumers and employees respond to AI-generated influencers, deepfakes, AI leaders, and AI supervision systems, with a particular interest in authenticity, trust, and wellbeing.
The publications below represent some of the questions that have kept me busy over the years; and in many cases, the questions I am still trying to answer.

title: My teaching
text:
Teaching philosophy
The classroom is where I meet people. I feel fortunate to have met talented and passionate students who have taught me as much as I have taught them. I aim to create a student-centered and industry-connected learning environment where students actively construct knowledge through discussion, collaboration, and reflection. My teaching follows an interactive learning approach using three classroom formats: L–C (Lecturer–Class Reflection), L–I–I–C (Lecturer–Individual–Individual–Class Reflection), and L–G–G–C (Lecturer–Group–Group–Class Reflection). Rather than relying on conventional group presentations, students engage in simulations of real business activities to apply concepts in authentic contexts. I also integrate industry perspectives by inviting experts to share their experiences and discuss current business challenges with students.


Courses taught

Introduction to Public Relations - PR202DE01
Crisis Management - PR402DE01
Fundamentals of Business and Management - PR201DE02
Media Audience Research - TT301DE02
Principles of Marketing - 71MRKT30013
Consumer Behavior - 71MRKT40073
Business Analytics - 71BUSI30053
Introduction to Marketing
E-Commerce
Strategic Marketing
Introduction to International Business



Industry engagement
In 2026, I invited C-level executives, senior managers, and academics from the following organizations to share their real-world experiences with my students.

Milwaukee Tool Vietnam — Ms. Huong Phan, Marketing Manager
Saigon Telecommunications Corporation — Ms. Ngoc Dung, Chief Accountant
Teknologi MARA University (Shah Alam) — Mr. Najmi, Senior Lecturer
Vinasun Taxi Corporation — Mr. Du Tran, Regional Manager



Student Supervision

In 2026, I am supervising 11 undergraduate students for their bachelor’s theses.
To date, I have supervised more than 30 undergraduate students on their bachelor’s theses and 11 students during their internships.



Teaching materials
I believe slides should support learning, not replace it. Hence, I prefer a minimalist design that encourages discussion and critical thinking. Below is one sample.

title: Freelance Projects
text: Not everything I do ends up in a journal article or classroom. Along the way, I have worked on a range of freelance projects, each with its own story, challenges, and lessons.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
First Project
In 2011, I started a speaker team at an English center to create opportunities for learners to practice English outside the classroom. What began with three members grew into a 20-member team by 2013 and achieved a student satisfaction rating of 4.5 out of 5. It was one of the first projects that showed me how ideas can grow when people share a common purpose.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Second Project
In 2012, I managed a Couchsurfing house that hosted travelers from across the globe. For a few years, it became a place where people from different countries, cultures, and walks of life crossed paths. Every guest brought a new story, making the house feel like a small window into a much larger world.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Third Project
In 2014, I began my freelance marketing journey by working with beauty vlogger Góc của Rư. My role focused on offline activities, including planning agendas for fan meetings and makeup classes. Behind every successful event are many small pieces working together, and I was fortunate to be one of them. The experience gave me my first glimpse into influencer marketing long before it became a mainstream industry.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Fourth Project
From late 2014 to 2015, I began organizing marketing events on my own. One of my first projects was an outdoor event for Cali Elementary School, which welcomed nearly 80 guests. I coordinated external partners, including a live band and an MC, and worked with local authorities to secure the necessary permits. The event was well received, leading the school owner to invite me to organize two additional events at her other campuses in Go Vap and Thu Duc.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Fifth Project
In 2015, I noticed how much Vietnamese learners enjoyed practicing English through real conversations with foreigners. Inspired by this, I created a local version of The Amazing Race and invited 24 participants to join the adventure. Divided into four teams, they solved clues that guided them across five locations in Ho Chi Minh City, while working together with foreign travelers to complete a series of challenges. Looking back, it was one of my favorite projects because it combined learning, exploration, and human connection into a single experience.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Sixth Project
In 2015, I had the opportunity to lead the organization of the 5-year anniversary celebration for the non-profit language center Leaf Pagoda. From early planning and preparation to coordinating activities on the day itself, I was involved in every stage of the event. It was one of the first projects that showed me how many moving pieces come together behind the scenes to create a memorable experience.

title: Freelance Projects
text:
Other Projects
Beyond these six projects, I explored many other paths as well: teaching on television, helping a clinic attract investment, managing a fast-food store, and directing a stage play, among others. Each experience added a different chapter to my story. Some lasted only a few months, while others changed the direction of my journey entirely. Together, they form a collection of memories, lessons, and adventures that continue to shape the person I am today.





One project that remains memorable was directing the stage play Wukong Re-Spring. We reimagined a familiar story, assembled a diverse creative team, and transformed a simple concept into a live performance.

title: Moments
text:
In the spotlight
Every chapter leaves a few footprints. These are some moments where my work, ideas, and experiences have been shared with people.

title: Todd Cao - Lecturer in Marketing and Management
text:
I’m Todd Cao, a Vietnamese PhD Candidate in Management at the University of Southern Queensland, Australia and Open University Vietnam, preparing to complete my doctorate and begin the next chapter as a lecturer.
I am curious about how consumption can make consumers happy; in other words, consumer well-being. I am now expanding into the study of Deepfake influencers, who I believe can also bring joy to people’s lives. Alongside my research, I bring more than ten years of teaching experience across companies and universities, supported by a career that spans industries from education to the non-profit sectors.
Outside academia, I am an amateur guitarist with a love for dogs. It is my time to step up as a man again who provides for those I love, including my family and two homeless children, Atri and Axet.To make this website more personal, I’ve prepared a quick video introduction.
